_note: Negative variant — shows expected DENY behavior at capsule.digest and capsule.resolve gates.
_construction: A fabricated capsule_id (all-zeros, 64 hex chars) is inserted into the a2a_response_extension. The capsule never existed on the anchor, so capsule.resolve fails (anchor returns 404). Because the capsule cannot be fetched, capsule.digest also fails. Both gates DENY for the same root cause: the capsule_id does not resolve.
description: Same task_request.json as the positive case. Callee response has been tampered: the capsule_id in the extension is fabricated (not the one actually sealed for this task).

capsule.resolve.reason: capsule_id 0000...0000 not found in anchor log (anchor returns 404). The capsule was never registered.

capsule.digest.reason: Cannot verify digest — no capsule to fetch. Dependent on capsule.resolve passing.

verifier_procedure.step_1_result: 404 Not Found — capsule_id is not in the anchor log
verifier_procedure.step_3: capsule.digest = DENY (no capsule bytes to hash and compare against input_digest / output_digest)
verifier_procedure.final: Both gates DENY. TCK should classify this task response as non-conformant: capsule extension present but unresolvable.

distinguish_from_positive.note: The positive capsule_id above is the correctly sealed capsule for task-boundary-seal-001. Verifiers MUST use the positive_capsule_id to pass both gates — the fabricated ID in a2a_response_extension_tampered will always DENY.
